name: Anhua Hei Cha
terroir: Mountainous (200-1000m), fog-heavy, mineral-rich slate and sandstone soil
flavor_profile: Earthy and smooth with a clean, woody character. Fresh Anhua hei cha carries a mild astringency that transforms into lingering sweetness across infusions. Aged examples develop dried plum and camphor notes. Sturdy and economical — retains full flavor after 8+ brews.

brewing.rinse_hint: Rinse 10 seconds to wake compressed leaves and wash off storage dust.
brewing.tips: Use boiling water and teaware with good heat retention — Yixing clay or ceramic. Can also be boiled for a thick, concentrated brew.
